description: Definition of a VibeKB installation. VibeKB owns .vibekb/ and nothing else outright: the runtime, reference docs, prompts, and starter model are all consolidated under .vibekb/ via the payload map below (src is repo-root-relative in VibeKB's own tree — what the binary embeds — and dest is where it is written in a target repository). Files outside .vibekb/ are optional adapters: 'namespaced' files VibeKB owns inside a shared tool directory, and 'managed-block' sections inserted into shared, user-owned files. This file is the single source of truth for the installed file set — the native Go installer parses it (from its embedded copy). See docs/REPOSITORY_SAFETY.md.

payload.note: VibeKB-owned, consolidated under .vibekb/. Copied on install and refreshed on upgrade. Directory srcs are copied recursively, preserving their internal structure under dest. These are safe to overwrite on upgrade because they are VibeKB's runtime and reference, not your application's code.

integrations.note: Optional adapters outside .vibekb/. 'namespaced' adapters are VibeKB-owned files that live inside a shared tool directory (safe to create and replace, never touching sibling files). 'managed-block' adapters edit a shared, user-owned file only inside a single marked block, preserving everything else byte-for-byte. Selection: by default a namespaced adapter installs only when its tool directory already exists, and a managed-block adapter applies only when its target file already exists; `--integrate <name,...>` forces specific adapters; `--no-integrations` / `--knowledge-only` skip them all.

block_markers.note: Managed-block delimiters inserted into shared files. The pair is matched on install to update in place; content outside it is never altered. version lets a future format change be detected and re-rendered.

manifest_file.note: The installation manifest VibeKB writes to record ownership, provenance, and hashes so upgrades, doctor, migration, and uninstall are safe. Stores no absolute paths.

starter_model.note: The fresh .vibekb/ model is produced from a single canonical, language-neutral definition. In a target repository the definition is installed at .vibekb/runtime/template/starter so `php .vibekb/runtime/tools/vibekb.php bootstrap` can repair the workspace from the same data the native Go installer embeds. The model files land directly under .vibekb/ (root), NOT under runtime/.

legacy.note: Paths a pre-2.0 (root-level) VibeKB install placed in a target repository. Migration relocates recognised, unmodified copies under .vibekb/ and leaves anything ambiguous in place with a report. Never delete on filename alone.

not_installed.note: Deliberately excluded from a target repository. The marketing homepage (index.php, assets/) would collide with the target application's own root; VibeKB's own model, examples, generated docs, host-specific deploy config, and the Go developer CLI (a separately distributed binary) are VibeKB-repo-only.
